mechanism_narrative: Sustained employment growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy. This robust labor market supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth rate of PAYEMS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in retail sales suggests weakening consumer demand and confidence, which directly impacts corporate revenues and profit margins. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors price in slower economic activity.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the RSAFS trigger condition is met, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is maintained at a low level while housing starts show consistent growth, it signals an accommodative monetary environment that stimulates real estate and broader economic activity. This combination fosters investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day period.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% AND HOUST increases by > 5% month-over-month
regime_hint: early-cycle economic expansion with loose monetary policy
failure_condition: The joint condition of low FEDFUNDS and rising HOUST does not result in statistically significant positive SPX 60-day returns, or the average return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment, any significant month-over-month decrease in the unemployment rate signals a nascent labor market recovery. This improvement boosts consumer confidence and corporate outlook, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (specifically when UNRATE is above 6.0%)
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances during identified high unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production indicates a significant slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This directly translates into weaker corporate earnings and reduced future growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by >0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real GDP per capita reflects a healthy and expanding economy with increasing productivity and living standards. This provides a strong fundamental basis for corporate profitability and sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a longer 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth does not correlate positively with SPX 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation is rising significantly while the unemployment rate is low, it indicates an overheating economy with potential wage-price spirals. This scenario typically prompts the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy aggressively, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by > 0.3% month-over-month AND UNRATE < 5.0%
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL rises significantly and UNRATE is low, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the Federal Funds Rate signals a tightening monetary policy stance by the central bank. This action raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances during identified monetary tightening cycles.
